There's a balance between two poles I find it existentially important to wobble around in life as a human being on Earth:

- You need to study and understand your [[Bodymind|bodymind]] as a _tool_ for experiencing the world — this allows you to become progressively more skilled in [[Lifecraft|lifecraft]] day-to-day
  - Very objective, very empirical
  - A cycle of: try something -> analyze results -> form a model -> test the model with experiments -> ...

- I am a being who can imagine, and go out into the world and make things I'm imagining happening — I can learn about invisible forces that move stuff around in the world, nebulas exist! And so does love, and tangerines, and chocolate. Sometimes I feel so connected to the plants and animals and oceans on this planet I feel like every cell in my body is vibrating at 1Hz, a deep loving resonant note from the heart of the universe. How is this not magic?

An example of the wobble is my relationship with tarot. I do not believe tarot cards show you future events. I _do_ believe tarot cards are magic. The magic though is about archetypes, through art and ritual and paper and numbers and systems (magic!) you coax narratives that were invisible to your conscious mind up from the dark ocean of your subconscious. You make powerful and invisible stories suddenly legible, and you can take meaningful action on what was revealed that improves your life. Is it magic? No, of course not. But is it magic? Yes, clearly.

If that last paragraph frustrated you, you need to work on your wobble!
